title: Theo's Big Helper Day
synopsis: When Theo wants to help Mama cook but feels too little, he discovers that even small helpers can make a big difference in the kitchen.

pages.text: Theo was excited! Mama was making pancakes for breakfast. The kitchen smelled sweet and warm. "I want to help!" said Theo, jumping up and down. But when he looked at the tall counter, he felt sad. Everything was so high up.

illustration.description: A small boy with curly brown hair and big brown eyes jumping excitedly in a bright yellow kitchen. His mama, a woman with dark skin and natural hair in a colorful apron, stands at a tall counter mixing batter. The counter looks very high compared to little Theo. Warm morning sunlight streams through the window.
illustration.alt: Theo jumping excitedly while Mama cooks at the tall kitchen counter

pages.text: "I'm too little," Theo said with a frown. His shoulders drooped and he felt scared that he couldn't help. Mama looked down and saw Theo's sad face. "Oh sweetie," she said gently. "Let me think about this."

illustration.description: Theo looking up sadly with drooped shoulders and a disappointed frown, standing next to the kitchen counter that towers above him. His mama bends down slightly with a caring expression, one hand on her hip and the other holding a wooden spoon. The kitchen has bright red and yellow accents with textured walls.
illustration.alt: Theo looking sad and small next to the tall counter while Mama looks down caringly

pages.text: Mama's eyes lit up with an idea. "Wait right here!" she said happily. She went to the closet and brought back Theo's special step stool. It was bright blue with red handles. "This will help you reach!" Mama said with a big smile.

illustration.description: Mama carrying a bright blue step stool with red handles, her face beaming with joy and excitement. Theo watches with curious eyes and a small hopeful smile starting to form. The closet door is open behind them, and the kitchen background shows colorful bowls and cooking supplies on the textured countertops.
illustration.alt: Mama carrying Theo's blue step stool with a happy expression

pages.text: Theo climbed up on his step stool. Now he could see everything! "I'm tall!" he giggled happily. Mama gave him a big wooden spoon. "You can stir the batter," she said. Theo felt proud and brave as he stirred round and round.

illustration.description: Theo standing proudly on his blue step stool at the counter, now at the perfect height, stirring pancake batter with a big wooden spoon. His face shows pure joy and concentration. Mama stands beside him with her hands gently guiding. The kitchen counter has flour, eggs, and a mixing bowl with golden batter.
illustration.alt: Theo standing on his step stool happily stirring pancake batter

pages.text: "Perfect!" said Mama as Theo stirred. Then Papa came into the kitchen with baby sister Zara. "Look at my big helper!" Papa said with a huge grin. Theo felt so happy and important. He was helping make breakfast for his whole family!

illustration.description: Papa, a tall man with glasses and a warm smile, enters carrying baby Zara who has chubby cheeks and wispy hair. Theo beams with pride on his step stool while stirring. Mama claps her hands together happily. The kitchen feels warm and cozy with morning light, and steam rises from the pancake pan on the stove.
illustration.alt: Papa holding baby Zara while praising Theo who stirs proudly on his step stool

pages.text: Soon the pancakes were ready! The family sat together at their round table. "These are the best pancakes ever," said Papa. "That's because Theo helped make them," said Mama. Theo felt so proud and happy. He wasn't too little after all - he was just the right size to be a wonderful helper!

illustration.description: The family sitting around a cheerful round table with a stack of golden pancakes in the center. Theo sits proudly with a big smile, Papa adjusts his glasses while taking a bite, and Mama holds baby Zara in a high chair. The dining area has warm orange walls and a window showing a sunny day outside with textured, paper-like quality.
illustration.alt: The happy family enjoying pancakes together at their round dining table

metadata.illustrationStyle: Bright, cheerful cartoon style with expressive faces. Bold primary colors. Rounded, friendly shapes. Slightly textured backgrounds like construction paper.

questions.question: How did Theo feel when he couldn't reach the counter?
questions.explanation: Theo felt sad because he thought he was too little to help cook with Mama.

questions.question: What did Mama bring to help Theo?
questions.explanation: Mama brought Theo's special blue step stool so he could reach the counter safely.

questions.question: What job did Theo do to help make breakfast?
questions.explanation: Theo used a big wooden spoon to stir the pancake batter round and round.

questions.question: How did Theo feel at the end of the story?
questions.explanation: Theo felt proud and happy because he helped make the best pancakes ever for his family.

pages.title: Fill in the Missing Words
pages.instructions: Help complete the story by choosing the right words!
pages.text: Theo wanted to help Mama make _____ for breakfast. He felt sad because the counter was too _____ for him to reach. Mama brought his special _____ stool so he could help stir the batter.

pages.title: Feelings Match
pages.instructions: Match how Theo felt at different parts of the story!
pages.text: At first, Theo felt _____ because he couldn't reach. When Mama brought the step stool, he felt _____. After helping cook, Theo felt _____ of what he did.
